what_it_does: Implements StreamInitDiscoverer, an adapter that spawns a short live session, reads its newline-delimited JSON stdout, finds the first event matching a configurable filter, and parses that init self-declaration into CapabilityEntry rows using a data-driven field-to-kind map. It also captures any unmapped event fields as a gap-surfacing sdk_event entry rather than dropping them.
observations: The adapter is selected when DiscoverySource.type == 'stream-init'.
It replaces a {binary} token in the source command with the resolved executable path.
It reads stdout line-by-line as NDJSON and returns the first dict whose top-level fields match event_filter.
Parsing uses field_kind_map to decide which event fields are capability collections and which CapabilityEntry.kind they produce.
Metadata fields declared in metadata_fields are skipped and not treated as gaps.
Unmapped fields are captured in a single 'sdk_event/init.unmapped' CapabilityEntry with raw_extra and a fail-loud note.
If field_kind_map is empty but the event carries fields, it raises a RuntimeError.
CapabilityEntry ids are formatted as f'{kind}/{name}'.
_members handles dict keys, list[str], list[dict] with 'name' or first string value, and bare strings.
The module claims to contain no platform-name literals and to be verified with a stub transport; live spawn is lead-only.
standouts: Fail-loud behavior when no matching init event is found or when field_kind_map is empty against a populated event.
Unmapped init fields are surfaced as a curated gap artifact instead of being silently dropped.
Generic, shape-driven member extraction with no hard-coded platform/tool names.
conventions: Heavy structured docstrings with level/lane annotations.
Data-driven configuration via DiscoverySource attributes (command, event_filter, field_kind_map, metadata_fields, fail_loud, timeout_s, etc.).
Uses getattr(src, 'attr', default) for optional source fields.
Static helper methods for pure functions (_first_matching_event, _members).
Type hints and from __future__ import annotations.
concerns: _members fallback to the first string value in a dict item can be nondeterministic when multiple string fields exist.
All unmapped fields are collapsed into one sdk_event entry, which may obscure individual field identities in downstream queries.
event_filter matching is shallow (top-level keys only) and uses exact equality, so nested or partial matches are not supported.
fail_loud only raises when field_kind_map is empty, not when some fields are mapped and others are unmapped.
notes: A data-driven, platform-agnostic bridge from a live session's init NDJSON self-declaration to a normalized capability registry, with explicit gap-surfacing for unknown fields.
questions: What is the full schema of DiscoverySource expected by this adapter?
How is field_kind_map populated and versioned for each platform?
What fields does CapabilityEntry expose beyond those used here?
What is the contract of SubprocessAdapter.run_capture (return type, exception behavior)?
Why is fail_loud gated on an empty field_kind_map rather than any unmapped field?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it generically captures and parses an init event into capability rows while refusing to silently drop unknown fields.
apparent_themes: Live self-declaration as the source of truth.
Gap curation through explicit sdk_event artifacts.
intention_signals: The code is trying to create a reusable, policy-aware adapter that turns any platform's live init announcement into a curated capability ledger without hard-coding platform specifics, while forcing unresolved fields into the open for human or automated curation.
novelty: It encodes a 'no-self-report=gap' policy by materializing unmapped init event fields as a first-class CapabilityEntry rather than ignoring them, making missing mappings visible in the ledger.
contribution: Provides a generic, configurable mechanism to discover capabilities from a live session's init event and to surface unmapped self-declared fields as explicit gap entries for downstream curation.
summary_for_embedding: StreamInitDiscoverer is a data-driven adapter that spawns a scratch session, reads its NDJSON init event, and converts configured event fields into CapabilityEntry rows while capturing unmapped fields as a gap-surfacing sdk_event entry.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to normalize a platform's live runtime self-declaration into a platform-agnostic capability registry without silently dropping unknown fields.

symbols.does: Defines the stream-init discovery adapter and its generic parse/discover helpers.
symbols.description: StreamInitDiscoverer is a stateless adapter selected by DiscoverySource.type == 'stream-init'. It exposes discover() to obtain a live init event and parse() to turn that event into CapabilityEntry rows using source-provided maps, plus static helpers for NDJSON matching and collection member extraction.

symbols.does: Spawns a short scratch session and returns the first init event matching the source filter.
symbols.description: Builds the spawn command by substituting {binary}, invokes it through SubprocessAdapter with configurable timeout/stderr/stdin, scans NDJSON stdout, and returns the first dict matching event_filter. Fails loudly if no match is found or the command is empty.

symbols.does: Scans NDJSON lines and returns the first object whose top-level fields satisfy event_filter.
symbols.description: Iterates over non-empty stdout lines, parses JSON, skips non-dict and non-JSON chatter, and returns the first dict where every key/value in event_filter matches exactly. Returns None if no event qualifies.

symbols.does: Converts a matched init event into CapabilityEntry rows according to field_kind_map.
symbols.description: For each event field, skips metadata fields, maps declared collection fields to one CapabilityEntry per member using kind/name ids, and aggregates any unmapped fields into a single 'sdk_event/init.unmapped' entry with raw_extra and a descriptive note. Raises if field_kind_map is empty while the event carries fields and fail_loud is true.

symbols.does: Yields member names from a collection field value generically.
symbols.description: Handles dict keys, list elements (strings or dicts with a 'name' field, falling back to the first string value), and bare strings, yielding each as a member name without platform-specific literals.
